title: Pulls and impacts on the Asimov dataset

posts.name: Luca Guzzi
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I am trying to compute the pulls for the Asimov dataset on a simple expo+gaussian model with 50 nuisance parameters. I run the commands I found on the twiki
>> combine -M FitDiagnostics -d /gwpool/users/lguzzi/Tau3Mu/2017_2018/combine_test/T3MuCombine/T3MCombineAll/March_16_2022/datacards/W/CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.txt -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --plots --rMin "-1" --rMax "10" --minos all --setParameterRanges $PARS
>> python $CMSSW_BASE/src/HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit/test/diffNuisances.py -a fitDiagnostics.root -g plots.root
>> text2workspace.py /gwpool/users/lguzzi/Tau3Mu/2017_2018/combine_test/T3MuCombine/T3MCombineAll/March_16_2022/datacards/W/CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.txt -m 1.777 -o CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -D data_obs
>> combineTool.py -M Impacts -d CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -m 1.777 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --doInitialFit --robustFit=1 --rMin "-1" --rMax "10"
>> combineTool.py -M Impacts -d CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -m 1.777 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --doFits --parallel 5 --rMin "-1" --rMax "10"
>> combineTool.py -M Impacts -d CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -m 1.777 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --rMin "-1" --rMax "10" -o impacts.json
>> plotImpacts.py -i impacts.json -o impacts

The results I get are shown in Fig. 46 [1] (page 56). For the case r=0, many low-impact parameters have a one-sided impact, is this expected? Should I expect a regular behaviour as for the “real data” case (see for example Fig. 47 of [1] at page 58)?
thanks,
Luca
[1] https://gitlab.cern.ch/tdr/notes/AN-20-127/-/blob/master/build/AN-20-127_temp.pdf

Dear experts,
I am trying to compute the pulls for the Asimov dataset on a simple expo+gaussian model with 50 nuisance parameters. I run the commands I found on the twiki

>> combine -M FitDiagnostics -d /gwpool/users/lguzzi/Tau3Mu/2017_2018/combine_test/T3MuCombine/T3MCombineAll/March_16_2022/datacards/W/CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.txt -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --plots --rMin "-1" --rMax "10" --minos all --setParameterRanges $PARS
>> python $CMSSW_BASE/src/HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit/test/diffNuisances.py -a fitDiagnostics.root -g plots.root
>> text2workspace.py /gwpool/users/lguzzi/Tau3Mu/2017_2018/combine_test/T3MuCombine/T3MCombineAll/March_16_2022/datacards/W/CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.txt -m 1.777 -o CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -D data_obs
>> combineTool.py -M Impacts -d CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -m 1.777 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --doInitialFit --robustFit=1 --rMin "-1" --rMax "10"
>> combineTool.py -M Impacts -d CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -m 1.777 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --doFits --parallel 5 --rMin "-1" --rMax "10"
>> combineTool.py -M Impacts -d CMS_T3MSignal_13TeV_W_Combined.root -m 1.777 -t -1 --expectSignal 0 --rMin "-1" --rMax "10" -o impacts.json
>> plotImpacts.py -i impacts.json -o impacts

The results I get are shown in Fig. 46 [1] (page 56). For the case r=0, many low-impact parameters have a one-sided impact, is this expected? Should I expect a regular behaviour as for the “real data” case (see for example Fig. 47 of [1] at page 58)?

thanks,
Luca

[1] https://gitlab.cern.ch/tdr/notes/AN-20-127/-/blob/master/build/AN-20-127_temp.pdf

posts.name: Andrew Gilbert
posts.text:
Hi Luca,
Sorry for the slow reply on this. I’m not sure exactly why these low ranked inputs give one-sided impacts. Could be some small jump the in fit minimum when doing the scan for the impacts (I noticed that the b-only Asimov doesn’t exactly recover r=0). Given the impacts are so small though (and the main ones are well behaved) I don’t think it’s anything to worry about.
cheers,
Andrew
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Luca,

Sorry for the slow reply on this. I’m not sure exactly why these low ranked inputs give one-sided impacts. Could be some small jump the in fit minimum when doing the scan for the impacts (I noticed that the b-only Asimov doesn’t exactly recover r=0). Given the impacts are so small though (and the main ones are well behaved) I don’t think it’s anything to worry about.

cheers,
Andrew

posts.name: Luca Guzzi
posts.text:
Hi Andrew,
thnks you for your reply. I actually found the problem. My model has 51 parameters, but the x variable was set to 40 bins (for plotting purposes only, the final fit is unbinned). I thought combine would set the binning correctly for the Asimov generation, but later I found out itì uses the one of the RooRealVar. Changing the binning to 100 fixed the problem (now background impacts are meaningful on the r=0 case, while signal impacts are zero within the fit precision, as expected)
best,
Luca

Changing the binning to 100 fixed the problem (now background impacts are meaningful on the r=0 case, while signal impacts are zero within the fit precision, as expected)

best,
Luca
